mechanism_narrative: A falling federal funds rate lowers the equity discount rate and supports valuation expansion, so sustained declines in FEDFUNDS predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in more than 55% of months when FEDFUNDS is declining for at least 2 consecutive observations, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in unemployment signals labor-market deterioration and higher recession risk, which should push S&P 500 returns lower over the next 20 trading days when UNRATE crosses above a recent rising threshold.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in at least 60% of cases, or if the median forward return is not below the unconditional median.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales and housing starts both strengthen, the signal reflects synchronized demand acceleration that improves earnings expectations and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint-high state of RSAFS and HOUST does not outperform either series alone on 5-day forward SPX returns, or if the joint signal has negative average return.

mechanism_narrative: A rising industrial production index indicates improving real output and capacity utilization, which should lift risk appetite and produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward SPX returns are not above baseline after 3-month upward trends in INDPRO, or if rolling 3-year rank correlation is consistently below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation shocks above the recent trend raise the probability of tighter policy and discount-rate repricing, so CPIAUCSL upside surprises predict negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL is above its 6-month average and FEDFUNDS is below 1.0%
regime_hint: inflation shock with policy normalization risk
failure_condition: Reject if inflation-upside episodes do not coincide with negative 20-day SPX returns more often than not, or if adding FEDFUNDS does not improve signal strength versus CPIAUCSL alone.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll growth combined with a falling unemployment rate indicates durable labor-market expansion, which supports consumption and earnings and should lead to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE fails to outperform single-series signals on 60-day SPX returns, or if the sign of the average return is negative.

mechanism_narrative: A housing-starts collapse below a prior low signals demand contraction and tighter financial conditions through weaker construction activity, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below its prior 3-month minimum
failure_condition: Reject if break-below episodes in HOUST are followed by positive 5-day SPX returns in more than 55% of cases, or if the average forward return is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production is rising while unemployment is falling, the economy is likely in a synchronized real-activity expansion that should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined INDPRO-up and UNRATE-down state does not produce higher 20-day SPX returns than either condition alone, or if the joint signal is not positive on average.
